2077083642354933978.headline: Youri Tielemans
2077083642354933978.body: Youri Tielemans joins ManUtd. Contract to 2031. It’s hard to describe just how proud I am to join Manchester United. Signing for such a special club feels incredible, it is the culmination of years of dedication since I first fell in love with football. I have had the privilege of experiencing success in the game and it has only increased my determination to achieve more. The ambition from everyone at the club is extremely clear; we are all determined to push for the biggest trophies over the coming years.

I’m joining an excellent group of goalkeepers and I’m really looking forward to all pushing each other to ensure that we maintain the highest standards, which this club demands. 2077810299030163502.body: Arsenal set to bid for Morgan Rogers after holding talks with both the player and Aston Villa. Club-to-club negotiations expected to accelerate following the World Cup. Arsenal also continue to explore Julian Alvarez. Player has made no secret of his desire to join Barcelona, but Andrea Berta is also in contact with former club Atletico Madrid. Although Atletico Madrid don't wish to sell, they are currently more inclined to do business with a non-La Liga rival. Barcelona haven't ruled out making a new bid, though. There are provisions for select UCL clubs to secure Alvarez for less than €500m clause. Bournemouth's Junior Kroupi is another profile on Arsenal's list, and appreciated by Spurs and several other top clubs. Bournemouth hoping to keep for at least another season. Arsenal's Bradley Barcola appreciation remains, but LFC more active currently. PSG could still become open to a sale this summer, as previously reported.🔴
2077810299030163502.raw_text:
Arsenal set to bid for Morgan Rogers after holding talks with both the player and Aston Villa. Club-to-club negotiations expected to accelerate following the World Cup.

Arsenal also continue to explore Julian Alvarez. Player has made no secret of his desire to join Barcelona, but Andrea Berta is also in contact with former club Atletico Madrid. Although Atletico Madrid don't wish to sell, they are currently more inclined to do business with a non-La Liga rival. Barcelona haven't ruled out making a new bid, though. There are provisions for select #UCL clubs to secure Alvarez for less than €500m clause.

Bournemouth's Junior Kroupi is another profile on Arsenal's list, and appreciated by Spurs and several other top clubs. Bournemouth hoping to keep for at least another season.

Arsenal's Bradley Barcola appreciation remains, but #LFC more active currently. PSG could still become open to a sale this summer, as previously reported.🔴

2077831004392219071.headline: Despite England
2077831004392219071.body: Thomas Tuchel has insisted that he “100 per cent” still wants to coach England at Euro 2028. England’s painful 2-1 semi-final defeat to Argentina on Wednesday night has put pressure on Tuchel, who signed a new contract in February to cover the next two years. Despite England’s loss — which they led 1-0 with five minutes remaining — Tuchel said afterwards that he does want to take the team forward. More from JackPittBrooke ⬇️ 🔗 nyti.ms/44CFDjd
2077831004392219071.raw_text:
Thomas Tuchel has insisted that he “100 per cent” still wants to coach England at Euro 2028.

England’s painful 2-1 semi-final defeat to Argentina on Wednesday night has put pressure on Tuchel, who signed a new contract in February to cover the next two years.

Despite England’s loss — which they led 1-0 with five minutes remaining — Tuchel said afterwards that he does want to take the team forward.

More from @JackPittBrooke ⬇️

🔗 nyti.ms/44CFDjd

2077800100030533972.headline: Harry Kane
2077800100030533972.body: Lots of people slagging off Harry Kane, saying he never turns up in the biggest games. Obviously struggled vs Argentina, but he's had a great World Cup, scoring six goals despite mediocre service much of the time. No service whatsoever yesterday. Criticised for dropping deep, criticised for staying up front, criticised for not single-handedly winning the game when the team has completely stopped playing. Think some people need to realise the problem with England isn't Harry Kane nytimes.com/athletic/7448908… Link Harry Kane and England fell short when it mattered most. He might not get another World Cup chance... There have been so many nearly moments but this might be the most painful yet for a striker who will be 36 at the 2030 tournament nytimes.com

2077694983272816695.headline: Didier Deschamps
2077694983272816695.body: Didier Deschamps has rarely been seen, at least from the outside, as a highly proactive tactician. For much of his reign, he has prioritised caution in his play, and has at times been accused by the French media of adopting a boring approach. In this tournament, he appeared to give players greater freedom, only for Spain to then remind everyone why Deschamps was attracted to the handbrake in the first place. His strengths lie more in his man-management, prioritising the squad environment across a long tournament. During his reign, Deschamps favoured players who can live together and tolerate a high-pressure, high-emotion zone for up to seven weeks, a period that includes the pre-tournament training camps. His players clearly bonded with him. Deschamps missed the final group game against Norway following his mother’s death, and when Mbappe scored against Sweden upon Deschamps’ return, he immediately ran to the coach to embrace him. For Deschamps, this is the end of his time in charge of France. His likely successor, the soon-to-be-anointed Zinedine Zidane, is arguably the most famous French footballer of all time. 📝 AdamCrafton_ 🔗 nyti.ms/4hfsAeT
2077694983272816695.raw_text:
Didier Deschamps has rarely been seen, at least from the outside, as a highly proactive tactician.

For much of his reign, he has prioritised caution in his play, and has at times been accused by the French media of adopting a boring approach.

In this tournament, he appeared to give players greater freedom, only for Spain to then remind everyone why Deschamps was attracted to the handbrake in the first place.

His strengths lie more in his man-management, prioritising the squad environment across a long tournament.

During his reign, Deschamps favoured players who can live together and tolerate a high-pressure, high-emotion zone for up to seven weeks, a period that includes the pre-tournament training camps.

His players clearly bonded with him. Deschamps missed the final group game against Norway following his mother’s death, and when Mbappe scored against Sweden upon Deschamps’ return, he immediately ran to the coach to embrace him. 

For Deschamps, this is the end of his time in charge of France. His likely successor, the soon-to-be-anointed Zinedine Zidane, is arguably the most famous French footballer of all time.

2077085170356613364.headline: Youri Tielemans
2077085170356613364.body: Aston Villa can confirm that Youri Tielemans has completed a permanent transfer to Manchester United. Having joined on a free transfer from Leicester City in 2023, Tielemans leaves Villa a UEFA Europa League winner, netting a stunning volley in the final that set Unai Emery’s side on their way to victory over Freiburg in May. Captain of his national team at the FIFA World Cup, Tielemans was a fan favourite in claret and blue, with his stellar performances earning him both Players’ Player and Supporters’ Player of the Season awards at the end of the 2024/25 campaign. He made a total of 134 appearances for Aston Villa in all competitions, scoring ten goals. A popular and highly-respected member of the dressing room, everyone at Aston Villa would like to thank Youri for his service to the club and wish him the very best in his future career.
2077085170356613364.raw_text:
Aston Villa can confirm that Youri Tielemans has completed a permanent transfer to Manchester United.

Having joined on a free transfer from Leicester City in 2023, Tielemans leaves Villa a UEFA Europa League winner, netting a stunning volley in the final that set Unai Emery’s side on their way to victory over Freiburg in May.

Captain of his national team at the FIFA World Cup, Tielemans was a fan favourite in claret and blue, with his stellar performances earning him both Players’ Player and Supporters’ Player of the Season awards at the end of the 2024/25 campaign.

He made a total of 134 appearances for Aston Villa in all competitions, scoring ten goals.

A popular and highly-respected member of the dressing room, everyone at Aston Villa would like to thank Youri for his service to the club and wish him the very best in his future career.

2077890591371784600.headline: Transfer update
2077890591371784600.body: The whole DNA thing from Tuchel clinches it for me: it's the story we tell ourselves that no England team can play like Spain (who of course didn't play like Spain for many years) and it simply isn't true: the 1990 England team, for example, had the most attacking quality on the ball in midfield of all the teams in that tournament - Gascoigne, Beardsley, Waddle - but we lost through bad luck - a deflection - and penalties. And then the story gets told and re-told, that England fail on the highest stage, or we can't play possession football, and the self-consciousness around that poisons things at the root. At some level many England managers, and Tuchel absolutely, think as a first principle that English players aren't touch/possession players - so create a squad to cope with that, ignoring or benching the players who are or might be those sort of players, because they don't fit the team model that comes with that cope - and then the team aren't good enough on the highest stage, yeah, because that first principle is wrong.

And then the story gets told and re-told, that England fail on the highest stage, or "we can't play possession football," and the self-consciousness around that poisons things at the root.  At some level many England managers, and Tuchel absolutely, think as a first principle that English players aren't touch/possession players - so create a squad to cope with that, ignoring or benching the players who are or might be those sort of players, because they don't fit the team model that comes with that cope - and then the team aren't good enough on the highest stage, yeah, because that first principle is wrong.

2077742330677731537.headline: When Harry Kane
2077742330677731537.body: When Harry Kane steps down as England’s No 9 … who on earth steps up? Another reason why Thomas Tuchel’s sub aberration is so costly. Why there is so much anger. Why it feels such a wasted opportunity. Realistically, this was England’s last shot with Kane, 33 in a fortnight, at the World Cup. England have some very good players, Bellingham, Saka, Gordon, Rice, Anderson etc plus some prospects coming through. But no No 9. (Nb Argentina fully deserved their victory).

2077260179637149938.headline: World Cup
2077260179637149938.body: Sage (Sage_FCB) Pep Guardiola on Spain taking a 2–0 lead over France in the FIFA World Cup semi-final. 🗣️ “This is the Spain I want the world to see. Brave, intelligent and fearless. They’re not just beating France,they’re controlling them, suffocating them and making one of the strongest teams in the world look completely lost.” “People said this young Spain side wouldn’t be ready for a World Cup semi-final. Look at them now. Every pass has purpose, every movement creates space and every player understands exactly what the team needs.” “France came with world-class players, but Spain have shown that football intelligence, courage and teamwork can make even the biggest stars look ordinary. This has been a football lesson.” “The way Spain have dominated possession, pressed relentlessly and punished every mistake has been exceptional. They aren’t relying on luck,they’re earning every single moment through superior football.” “At 2–0, this isn’t just a scoreline,it’s a statement to the rest of the world. If Spain continue playing with this confidence and quality, they’ll be the team everyone fears. This is football at the highest level.” —
2077260179637149938.raw_text:
Sage (@Sage_FCB)

Pep Guardiola on Spain taking a 2–0 lead over France in the FIFA World Cup semi-final.

🗣️ “This is the Spain I want the world to see. Brave, intelligent and fearless. They’re not just beating France,they’re controlling them, suffocating them and making one of the strongest teams in the world look completely lost.”

“People said this young Spain side wouldn’t be ready for a World Cup semi-final. Look at them now. Every pass has purpose, every movement creates space and every player understands exactly what the team needs.”

“France came with world-class players, but Spain have shown that football intelligence, courage and teamwork can make even the biggest stars look ordinary. This has been a football lesson.”

“The way Spain have dominated possession, pressed relentlessly and punished every mistake has been exceptional. They aren’t relying on luck,they’re earning every single moment through superior football.”

“At 2–0, this isn’t just a scoreline,it’s a statement to the rest of the world. If Spain continue playing with this confidence and quality, they’ll be the team everyone fears. This is football at the highest level.”







— https://nitter.net/Sage_FCB/status/2077127764541309296#m

2077453764110561506.headline: David Beckham
2077453764110561506.body: Before the Hand of God, and David Beckham’s red card, there was the controversy of the 1966 World Cup quarter-final between England and Argentina. In 90 minutes, 56 fouls were committed. Argentina captain Antonio Rattin refused to leave the pitch after being sent off, causing a delay of more than eight minutes. After England’s 1-0 win, Alf Ramsey described the Argentine players as “animals”. The Argentinians, for their part, claimed they had been cheated. On a recent visit to the UK’s National Archives, The Athletic saw a letter to the Foreign Office from the British Embassy in Buenos Aires in August 1966, referencing “a wave of anti-British feeling” in Argentina, including “hundreds of abusive telephone calls” to the embassy, protests outside the ambassador’s house and an “invasion” of a British exhibit at a show in Palermo. The newspapers, it said, were full of references linking England’s “dishonourable’ World Cup victory to its occupation of the Falklands. “Most Argentines quite sincerely believe that the way the whole tournament was managed, or mismanaged, was a dirty business and a stain on the British reputation for sportsmanship and honesty,” the embassy wrote. nyti.ms/4w5EWes
2077453764110561506.raw_text:
Before the Hand of God, and David Beckham’s red card, there was the controversy of the 1966 World Cup quarter-final between England and Argentina.

In 90 minutes, 56 fouls were committed. Argentina captain Antonio Rattin refused to leave the pitch after being sent off, causing a delay of more than eight minutes. After England’s 1-0 win, Alf Ramsey described the Argentine players as “animals”. The Argentinians, for their part, claimed they had been cheated.

On a recent visit to the UK’s National Archives, The Athletic saw a letter to the Foreign Office from the British Embassy in Buenos Aires in August 1966, referencing “a wave of anti-British feeling” in Argentina, including “hundreds of abusive telephone calls” to the embassy, protests outside the ambassador’s house and an “invasion” of a British exhibit at a show in Palermo.

The newspapers, it said, were full of references linking England’s “dishonourable’ World Cup victory to its occupation of the Falklands.

“Most Argentines quite sincerely believe that the way the whole tournament was managed, or mismanaged, was a dirty business and a stain on the British reputation for sportsmanship and honesty,” the embassy wrote.

nyti.ms/4w5EWes

2078012198325035149.headline: Thomas Tuchel
2078012198325035149.body: The Football Association was not shy in trumpeting its ambition when appointing Thomas Tuchel in October 2024. English football’s governing body said it was aiming for this Sunday’s World Cup final in New Jersey - hence it wanted an elite manager, someone who had won things, someone to give them the best chance of winning their first World Cup for 60 years. Its first target was Pep Guardiola, with whom there was a verbal agreement, before he decided to renew with Manchester City. That led it to Tuchel, a Champions League winner in his own right, instead. But England will not be in New Jersey this weekend. ✍️ JackPittBrooke, David_Ornstein and OliverKay 🔗 nyti.ms/4fbR9Xu
2078012198325035149.raw_text:
The Football Association was not shy in trumpeting its ambition when appointing Thomas Tuchel in October 2024.

English football’s governing body said it was aiming for this Sunday’s World Cup final in New Jersey - hence it wanted an elite manager, someone who had won things, someone to give them the best chance of winning their first World Cup for 60 years.

Its first target was Pep Guardiola, with whom there was a verbal agreement, before he decided to renew with Manchester City. That led it to Tuchel, a Champions League winner in his own right, instead.

But England will not be in New Jersey this weekend. 

✍️ @JackPittBrooke, @David_Ornstein and @OliverKay 

🔗 nyti.ms/4fbR9Xu

2078035039342727604.headline: World Cup
2078035039342727604.body: Thomas Tuchel was not appointed to carve out a new space for the England team, or to build something to last for years. He was appointed to win the World Cup. And there was genuine confidence he would. Many players were convinced by it and Jake Reid, the president of Sporting Kansas City told The Athletic that Tuchel and senior FA staff were even planning to get commemorative tattoos if England’s 60-year wait for a major trophy was ended. Was that a joke? Possibly, but the belief was real. JackPittBrooke, David_Ornstein and OliverKay go inside England’s World Cup ⤵️ 🔗 nyti.ms/4fbR9Xu
2078035039342727604.raw_text:
Thomas Tuchel was not appointed to carve out a new space for the England team, or to build something to last for years. He was appointed to win the World Cup.

And there was genuine confidence he would.

Many players were convinced by it and Jake Reid, the president of Sporting Kansas City told The Athletic that Tuchel and senior FA staff were even planning to get commemorative tattoos if England’s 60-year wait for a major trophy was ended.

Was that a joke? Possibly, but the belief was real.

@JackPittBrooke, @David_Ornstein and @OliverKay go inside England’s World Cup ⤵️

🔗 nyti.ms/4fbR9Xu

2078066420278030759.headline: Atlético Madrid
2078066420278030759.body: 🚨🔴⚪️ BREAKING: Atlético Madrid CEO Miguel Angel Gil says Julián Álvarez will NOT leave this summer. “My position is clear, the club’s position is clear. We’ve made it known to the player, his representatives, and the president of Barcelona”. “I have absolutely no doubt that Atlético is the right place in the world for Julián, and that Julián is the perfect centre-forward for Atlético Madrid. We want to keep him”. “I recently heard the president say that the offer he made to Atlético Madrid wasn’t unlimited. My only response is that our answer is unlimited”. “We do NOT want to transfer him”. “We didn’t accept an offer of €100 million, and we won’t accept one of €150 million or even €200 million”.
2078066420278030759.raw_text:
🚨🔴⚪️ BREAKING: Atlético Madrid CEO Miguel Angel Gil says Julián Álvarez will NOT leave this summer.

“My position is clear, the club’s position is clear. We’ve made it known to the player, his representatives, and the president of Barcelona”.

“I have absolutely no doubt that Atlético is the right place in the world for Julián, and that Julián is the perfect centre-forward for Atlético Madrid. We want to keep him”.

“I recently heard the president say that the offer he made to Atlético Madrid wasn’t unlimited. My only response is that our answer is unlimited”.

“We do NOT want to transfer him”.

“We didn’t accept an offer of €100 million, and we won’t accept one of €150 million or even €200 million”.

2078077727236640784.headline: Atletico Madrid
2078077727236640784.body: 🗣️ Atletico Madrid CEO Miguel Angel Gil says Julian Alvarez is not for sale. My opinion is clear. The club's will is clear. We have made this clear to the player and his agent, and to the president of Barcelona. I have no doubt that Atletico is the right place in the world for Julian, and Julian is the perfect striker for Atlético de Madrid. We want to keep him. I heard some statements from the Barcelona president [Joan Laporta] saying his offer wasn't open-ended. Our response is indeed open-ended: we don't want to transfer him. We didn't accept the €100 million offer, nor will we accept one of €150 million or €200 million.
2078077727236640784.raw_text:
🗣️ Atletico Madrid CEO Miguel Angel Gil says Julian Alvarez is not for sale. "My opinion is clear. The club's will is clear. We have made this clear to the player and his agent, and to the president of Barcelona. I have no doubt that Atletico is the right place in the world for Julian, and Julian is the perfect striker for Atlético de Madrid. We want to keep him.

"I heard some statements from the Barcelona president [Joan Laporta] saying his offer wasn't open-ended. Our response is indeed open-ended: we don't want to transfer him. We didn't accept the €100 million offer, nor will we accept one of €150 million or €200 million."

2078073662188425239.headline: Jarrod Bowen

2078063843385749579.headline: Michael Olise
2078063843385749579.body: 🚨🆕 (1/2) FC Bayern remain relaxed despite the exclusive report from L’Équipe. Internally, the stance has been discussed again today and fully approved by all decision-makers, including Uli Hoeneß: Michael Olise is not for sale. The club also believe they are in no position where they can be pressured. Bayern are fully aware that Real Madrid could become an option for Olise one day. That is no secret. As things stand, however, Bayern are planning with Olise 100%. Contract until 2029 has no exit-options. All doors remain closed at this stage. Bayern want to extend his contract and are prepared to make Olise one of the club’s top three earners. SkySportDE 🇫🇷

2078076370865786935.headline: Given England
2078076370865786935.body: The messaging was very clear in the aftermath of the Argentina defeat. Both Thomas Tuchel and the FA are committed to one another. There were clauses in Tuchel’s contract under which both parties could have agreed to his exit should England be knocked out at the group stage, the last 32 or the last 16. An exemption was subsequently made for the latter once it became clear that England were in line to face Mexico at the Azteca. Given England reached the semi-finals — for just the fourth time ever — none of those clauses were activated. So what now for Tuchel and England? JackPittBrooke, David_Ornstein and OliverKay go inside England’s World Cup. 🔗 nyti.ms/4fbR9Xu
2078076370865786935.raw_text:
The messaging was very clear in the aftermath of the Argentina defeat. Both Thomas Tuchel and the FA are committed to one another.

There were clauses in Tuchel’s contract under which both parties could have agreed to his exit should England be knocked out at the group stage, the last 32 or the last 16.

An exemption was subsequently made for the latter once it became clear that England were in line to face Mexico at the Azteca.

Given England reached the semi-finals — for just the fourth time ever — none of those clauses were activated.

So what now for Tuchel and England?

@JackPittBrooke, @David_Ornstein and @OliverKay go inside England’s World Cup.

🔗 nyti.ms/4fbR9Xu

2078112094155616445.headline: Atlético Madrid
2078112094155616445.body: 🚨🔴⚪️ Atlético Madrid CEO Gil: “Julián has been poorly advised since the season ended, and that, in a way, is what makes me believe that his performances and his attitude will remain exactly as they have always been: those of a very good player, a good person, and a true professional” “The truth is that we know Julián. We’ve worked with him over these past two seasons, and we have absolutely no doubts about his character or his professionalism”. “I think both the player and the fans have it very easy. Everything depends on the player’s attitude and, of course, his performances. In the end, Atlético Madrid knows exactly what it wants. It wants committed players and players who deliver on the pitch”. “That’s why I believe every Atlético Madrid fan’s dream, mine included, is to see Julián scoring goals for Atlético Madrid and winning trophies with Atlético Madrid. And I truly believe that’s exactly what’s going to happen”.
2078112094155616445.raw_text:
🚨🔴⚪️ Atlético Madrid CEO Gil: “Julián has been poorly advised since the season ended, and that, in a way, is what makes me believe that his performances and his attitude will remain exactly as they have always been: those of a very good player, a good person, and a true professional”

“The truth is that we know Julián. We’ve worked with him over these past two seasons, and we have absolutely no doubts about his character or his professionalism”.

“I think both the player and the fans have it very easy. Everything depends on the player’s attitude and, of course, his performances. In the end, Atlético Madrid knows exactly what it wants. It wants committed players and players who deliver on the pitch”.

“That’s why I believe every Atlético Madrid fan’s dream, mine included, is to see Julián scoring goals for Atlético Madrid and winning trophies with Atlético Madrid. And I truly believe that’s exactly what’s going to happen”.

2078150205594034301.headline: Morgan Rogers
2078150205594034301.body: Morgan Rogers is Arsenal's top target and club-to-club talks are already underway, as revealed yesterday.⬇️ Ben Jacobs (JacobsBen) Arsenal set to bid for Morgan Rogers after holding talks with both the player and Aston Villa. Club-to-club negotiations expected to accelerate following the World Cup. Arsenal also continue to explore Julian Alvarez. Player has made no secret of his desire to join Barcelona, but Andrea Berta is also in contact with former club Atletico Madrid. Although Atletico Madrid don't wish to sell, they are currently more inclined to do business with a non-La Liga rival. Barcelona haven't ruled out making a new bid, though. There are provisions for select UCL clubs to secure Alvarez for less than €500m clause. Bournemouth's Junior Kroupi is another profile on Arsenal's list, and appreciated by Spurs and several other top clubs. Bournemouth hoping to keep for at least another season. Arsenal's Bradley Barcola appreciation remains, but LFC more active currently. PSG could still become open to a sale this summer, as previously reported.🔴 —
2078150205594034301.raw_text:
Morgan Rogers is Arsenal's top target and club-to-club talks are already underway, as revealed yesterday.⬇️


Ben Jacobs (@JacobsBen)

Arsenal set to bid for Morgan Rogers after holding talks with both the player and Aston Villa. Club-to-club negotiations expected to accelerate following the World Cup.

Arsenal also continue to explore Julian Alvarez. Player has made no secret of his desire to join Barcelona, but Andrea Berta is also in contact with former club Atletico Madrid. Although Atletico Madrid don't wish to sell, they are currently more inclined to do business with a non-La Liga rival. Barcelona haven't ruled out making a new bid, though. There are provisions for select #UCL clubs to secure Alvarez for less than €500m clause.

Bournemouth's Junior Kroupi is another profile on Arsenal's list, and appreciated by Spurs and several other top clubs. Bournemouth hoping to keep for at least another season.

Arsenal's Bradley Barcola appreciation remains, but #LFC more active currently. PSG could still become open to a sale this summer, as previously reported.🔴







— https://nitter.net/JacobsBen/status/2077810299030163502#m

The Athletic reported on Sunday that Villa were in the process of finalising the move and he now moves to Villa Park on a long-term deal. 2078166306369511936.raw_text:
Aston Villa have announced the signing of Johan Manzambi from Freiburg.

The Athletic reported on Sunday that Villa were in the process of finalising the move and he now moves to Villa Park on a long-term deal.

Newcastle United had reached an agreement in principle with Freiburg to sign Manzambi, though the midfielder had yet to decide on his preferred destination, allowing Villa to step up their interest.

2079317819796197489.headline: Enzo Fernández
2079317819796197489.body: 🚨🇦🇷 Enzo Fernández statement. “As time passes, you realize there is something much bigger than a result. For years, this group has represented our country in the best possible way. It teaches that competing isn’t just about winning, but about giving everything for the shirt and never giving up. Being part of this group, which has always stood up, competed to the very end, and defended these colors with pride, humility, and commitment, is something I’ll always cherish. I want to thank all the Argentine fans. Thank you for always being there, for supporting us in every match, for your affection, for your unconditional support, and for making us feel at home no matter where we are in the world. Wearing my country’s shirt is the greatest honor of my career, and I will continue to give everything I have every time I’m called upon to defend it.
2079317819796197489.raw_text:
🚨🇦🇷 Enzo Fernández statement.

“As time passes, you realize there is something much bigger than a result.

"For years, this group has represented our country in the best possible way.

"It teaches that competing isn’t just about winning, but about giving everything for the shirt and never giving up. Being part of this group, which has always stood up, competed to the very end, and defended these colors with pride, humility, and commitment, is something I’ll always cherish.

"I want to thank all the Argentine fans. Thank you for always being there, for supporting us in every match, for your affection, for your unconditional support, and for making us feel at home no matter where we are in the world.

"Wearing my country’s shirt is the greatest honor of my career, and I will continue to give everything I have every time I’m called upon to defend it."

deferred.headline: Michael Olise
deferred.body: Michael Olise is set to stay; no exit is planned at this stage.
deferred.text:
🚨❌ FC Bayern have also received confirmation from Michael Olise’s camp that there have been no talks, meetings or negotiations with Real Madrid so far.
No offer from Real Madrid either. Bayern are not open to negotiations. Olise has not expressed any desire to leave.
Instead, Bayern are working behind the scenes on contract extension beyond 2029 and want to make him one of the club’s top earners.  🇫🇷

deferred.headline: Jones
deferred.body: Jones is set to stay at Liverpool; no exit is planned at this stage.
deferred.text:
Inter made a fresh approach for Curtis Jones last week asking whether a deal was possible for around €25m.
This was a conversation not a formal or written offer.
Liverpool made it clear no sale at this price, especially given numbers other midfielders are expected to be sold for this summer.
Liverpool are prepared to keep Jones unless a suitable bid arises, even if that means losing him on a free next summer.
Liverpool have never had concerns about Jones' attitude or professionalism despite Inter reapproaching the club, and growing interest elsewhere.
Understand the club's position has hardened as a result of Inter's latest contact. If the right offer emerges, a sale will be sanctioned, but Inter would have to significantly increase their valuation to get Liverpool to engage.
Worth noting, Liverpool are also comfortable with Jones staying, and if that happens, and he impresses Andoni Iraola, they could yet decide to engage in new contract talks.🔴
deferred.draft_caption:
📝 REPORTED: Jones (Liverpool)
Jones is set to stay at Liverpool; no exit is planned at this stage.

#FPLVortex #Transfers #LFC #PremierLeague

deferred.text:
